rationale: Task Manager (taskmgr.exe) was launched interactively by corp\Administrator from explorer.exe (EV-001), which is a plausible cover for a manual credential-dumping action. It subsequently opened lsass.exe (PID 856) with escalating access rights — 0x1400 and 0x1410 (EV-002, EV-003) for initial enumeration, then 0x1fffff (PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS) in A0 — and the call trace in A0 routes through dbgcore.dll, the standard MiniDumpWriteDump code path. This culminated in the confirmed creation of a lsass memory dump file at 'C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\2\lsass (2).DMP' (EV-004), which is direct evidence of credential material being written to disk. While Task Manager can legitimately create process dumps, the naming convention 'lsass (2).DMP' and the PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS grant to lsass are consistent with deliberate credential harvesting; the dump file on disk is the key confirmed indicator that sensitive credential data has been exfiltrated to a user-accessible temp path.
